content_markdown: Which of the following gives the conditions for maximum amplitude in forced, but damped, oscillations?

diagram_metadata.description: A table showing four combinations of driving frequency and damping levels.

options.label: A
options.text: Driving frequency: greater than natural frequency; Damping: as large as possible

options.label: B
options.text: Driving frequency: equal to natural frequency; Damping: as large as possible

options.label: C
options.text: Driving frequency: greater than natural frequency; Damping: as small as possible

options.label: D
options.text: Driving frequency: equal to natural frequency; Damping: as small as possible

vibe_explanation: Resonance occurs when the driving frequency is equal (or very close) to the natural frequency of the system, resulting in maximum energy transfer. Additionally, damping removes energy from the system; therefore, to achieve the absolute maximum amplitude, damping should be minimized so that energy is not dissipated as heat.
